title: Someone to Sit With
prose: It happens without you quite noticing it happen: somewhere in the last few weeks, there's stopped being a question of where to sit at meals, or who to partner with in class. A face that was a stranger on the train is just — someone you know now. A friend, if you're being honest about it.

It isn't dramatic. Nobody saves anybody from a troll or a burning tapestry. It's smaller than that: an inside joke about a professor's handwriting, a shared stash of Chocolate Frogs, someone saving you a seat without being asked.
Whatever else this year turns out to hold, that part, at least, already feels solid.

choices.label: Let it be exactly as unremarkable and important as it is.
